title: Building Scalable REST APIs with Node.js & Express
shortDesc: Learn how to architect production-ready REST APIs using Node.js, Express, and MongoDB with authentication middleware.
intro: When building a production REST API, scalability is not an afterthought — it's a design requirement. In this post, I walk through the architecture patterns I've refined after building dozens of Node.js APIs.

sections.heading: Project Structure
sections.code:
src/
├── controllers/   # Route handlers
├── middleware/    # Auth, validation, error
├── models/        # Mongoose schemas
├── routes/        # Express routers
├── services/      # Business logic
├── utils/         # Helpers, constants
└── app.js         # Entry point

sections.heading: Key Principles
bullets: Separate business logic from route handlers using a service layer
Use centralized error handling middleware — never swallow errors silently
Validate all incoming data at the boundary with Joi or Zod
Rate-limit public endpoints; use Redis for distributed rate limiting
Use MongoDB indexes strategically — analyze with explain()

sections.heading: Centralized Error Handler

title: Cisco VLAN Configuration: Step-by-Step Guide
shortDesc: A complete walkthrough of setting up VLANs on Cisco switches, including inter-VLAN routing and trunk ports.
intro: VLANs (Virtual Local Area Networks) are the backbone of enterprise network segmentation. This guide walks through creating, assigning, and routing between VLANs on Cisco switches using Packet Tracer.

sections.heading: Why Use VLANs?
bullets: Segment broadcast domains to reduce network congestion
Improve security by isolating sensitive departments

sections.heading: Inter-VLAN Routing (Router-on-a-Stick)
sections.code:
! On the router – subinterfaces for each VLAN
Router(config)# interface GigabitEthernet0/0
Router(config-if)# no shutdown

Router(config)# interface GigabitEthernet0/0.10
Router(config-subif)# encapsulation dot1Q 10
Router(config-subif)# ip address 192.168.10.1 255.255.255.0

Router(config)# interface GigabitEthernet0/0.20
Router(config-subif)# encapsulation dot1Q 20
Router(config-subif)# ip address 192.168.20.1 255.255.255.0

title: Laravel Sanctum: Complete API Auth Setup
shortDesc: Implementing token-based authentication using Laravel Sanctum for SPA and mobile app backends.
intro: Laravel Sanctum provides a featherweight authentication system for SPAs, mobile applications, and simple token-based APIs. Here's the complete setup from installation to protecting routes.

sections.heading: Full Sanctum Setup

title: Network Security Best Practices for SMEs
shortDesc: Essential network security measures for small businesses: firewalls, DMZ, ACLs, and monitoring tools.
intro: Small and medium businesses are increasingly targeted by cyberattacks precisely because they often lack enterprise-grade security. Here are the non-negotiable practices every SME network should implement.

numberedList.label: Perimeter Firewall with DMZ
numberedList.desc: Never expose internal servers directly to the internet. Place public-facing services in a DMZ zone.

numberedList.label: VLAN Segmentation
numberedList.desc: Isolate departments (HR, Finance, IT, Guest) into separate VLANs with restrictive inter-VLAN ACLs.

numberedList.label: Disable Telnet, use SSH
numberedList.desc: Telnet sends credentials in plain text. Always enforce SSH with key-based authentication on network devices.

numberedList.label: Network Monitoring
numberedList.desc: Deploy SNMP + Zabbix or Nagios. Alert on unusual traffic spikes that may indicate a breach or DDoS.

numberedList.label: Regular Firmware Updates
numberedList.desc: Cisco and other vendors regularly patch critical vulnerabilities. Schedule quarterly device updates.

title: Docker + Laravel: Production Deployment Guide
shortDesc: Containerizing a Laravel application with Docker, Nginx, and MySQL for a production-ready deployment.
intro: Containerizing a Laravel application makes deployments reproducible, portable, and scalable. Here's my production-ready docker-compose setup with Nginx, PHP-FPM, MySQL, and Redis.

sections.heading: docker-compose.yml
sections.code:
version: '3.9'
services:
  app:
    build: .
    volumes:
      - .:/var/www/html
    depends_on: [db, redis]

  nginx:
    image: nginx:alpine
    ports: ["80:80","443:443"]
    volumes:
      - .:/var/www/html
      - ./docker/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf

  db:
    image: mysql:8.0
    environment:
      MYSQL_DATABASE:      ${DB_DATABASE}
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
    volumes:
      - mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql

  redis:
    image: redis:alpine
    command: redis-server --requirepass ${REDIS_PASSWORD}

volumes:
  mysql_data:

title: ASP.NET Core Minimal APIs in 2025
shortDesc: Exploring the power of Minimal APIs in ASP.NET Core — clean, fast, and production-grade endpoint setup.
intro: Minimal APIs in ASP.NET Core strip away the boilerplate of controllers and offer a clean, fast way to build HTTP endpoints. They shine for microservices and lightweight APIs where every millisecond counts.
